title: coreopsis bicolor
intro: Coreopsis bicolor, also known as the tickseed or yellow coreopsis, is a flowering plant species native to the eastern United States. It is a perennial plant that belongs to the aster family and is known for its bright yellow and pink flowers, which are a popular choice for gardeners due to their vibrant colors and low maintenance requirements. The medicinal properties of Coreopsis bicolor include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. Coreopsis bicolor can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has been reported to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
